content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA with NNN positive integers - (A1,A2,…,AN)(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N)(A1​,A2​,…,AN​).

A partition of the array into subarrays refers to dividing the entire array into subarrays (ie. some continuous elements) such that each of the NNN elements is in exactly one subarray.

We call a partition of the array into subarrays as Valid, if this condition holds true: For all iii, if iii and i+1i+1i+1 are in the same subarray then Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​ must be divisible by AiA_iAi​.

Now, among all such Valid partitions, we are interested in the partition with the minimum number of subarrays. It can be proved that there is a unique such partition. We will be interested only with this partition in the problem.

We call SiS_iSi​ the index of the subarray in which element iii lies.

We now want to support some operations on the array. In particular, there are QQQ operations of the form -


111 iii  XXX - Change AiA_iAi​ to XXX
222  iii - Given iii , output the smallest jjj such that Si=SjS_i = S_jSi​=Sj​. That is, the leftmost index which belongs to the same subarray as the i-th element.
###Input:


First line will contain NNN and QQQ denoting the size of AAA and number of operations respectively.
The second line contains NNN values, the initial values of AAA.
The next QQQ lines contain the operations as stated above.
###Output:


The answer for each query of type 2 on a new line.
###Constraints


1≤N≤1061 \leq N \leq 10^61≤N≤106
1≤Q≤2∗1051 \leq Q \leq 2*10^51≤Q≤2∗105
1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N
1≤Ai,X≤1061 \leq A_i, X \leq 10^61≤Ai​,X≤106
###Subtasks


30 points :  Ai≠Ai+1A_i \neq A_{i+1}Ai​=Ai+1​ for 1≤i≤N−11 \leq i\leq N-11≤i≤N−1. This holds throughout, even after updates.
70 points : Original constraints
###Sample Input:

`5 5
2 2 7 14 14
1 1 3
1 2 6
2 2
2 4
2 5
`###Sample Output:

`1
3
3
`###Explanation:
Initially, A=[2,2,7,14,14]A=[2, 2, 7, 14, 14]A=[2,2,7,14,14] and S=[1,1,2,2,2]S=[1, 1, 2,  2, 2]S=[1,1,2,2,2] ( A2A_2A2​ is divisible by A1A_1A1​, A3A_3A3​ is not divisible by A2A_2A2​, A4A_4A4​ is divisible by A3A_3 A3​, A5A_5A5​ is divisible by A4A_4A4​ )

After the first operation A=[3,2,7,14,14]A=[3, 2, 7, 14, 14]A=[3,2,7,14,14]  and S=[1,2,3,3,3]S=[1, 2, 3,  3, 3]S=[1,2,3,3,3]  (A1A_1A1​ is 3 now and A2mod  A1≠0A_2 \mod A_1 ≠ 0A2​modA1​=0 now)

After the second operation A=[3,6,7,14,14]A=[3, 6, 7, 14, 14]A=[3,6,7,14,14] and S=[1,1,2,2,2]S=[1, 1, 2,  2, 2]S=[1,1,2,2,2]  (A2A_2A2​ is 6 now and A2A_2A2​ is divisible by A1A_1A1​ once again)

For the queries, we can look at the SiS_iSi​ value for the given index and find the smallest index with the same value. Note here that the exact value of SiS_iSi​ is irrelevant. We just want the first element of the same chain.
